question_text: An irregular stone is placed in a measuring cylinder with 250 cm³ of water. The water level rises to 340 cm³. If the stone has a mass of 645 g, what is its density? Would this stone sink in oil with a density of 0.9 g/cm³?
correct_answer: Density = 6.45 g/cm³; yes, it would sink because 6.45 g/cm³ > 0.9 g/cm³
explanation: The volume of the stone is 340 - 250 = 90 cm³. Using density = mass ÷ volume gives 645 ÷ 90 = 6.45 g/cm³. Since this is much greater than the oil's density of 0.9 g/cm³, the stone will definitely sink.
